title: juncus arcticus subsp. balticus
intro: Juncus arcticus subsp. balticus, also known as Baltic rush, is a subspecies of the Arctic rush plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Juncaceae). Traditionally, Baltic rush has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, and it has been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and support the immune system. Baltic rush can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and address specific health issues.
